edges.description: Altered retinol flux perturbs downstream embryonic retinoid signaling.
Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts ocular development.
Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts pulmonary development.
Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts diaphragm formation.
Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts cardiac morphogenesis.
Severe ocular developmental failure can produce absent globes.
Partial ocular developmental failure can produce abnormally small eyes.
Abnormal pulmonary development produces lung underdevelopment.
Abnormal diaphragm formation produces diaphragmatic hernia or eventration.
Abnormal cardiac morphogenesis produces congenital heart defects.

nodes.description: Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts cardiac morphogenesis and contributes to variable congenital heart malformations.

evidence.reference_title: RBP4 disrupts vitamin A uptake homeostasis in a STRA6-deficient animal model for Matthew-Wood syndrome.
evidence.snippet: These fatal consequences of Stra6 deficiency, including craniofacial and cardiac defects and microphthalmia, were largely alleviated by reducing embryonic Rbp4 levels by morpholino oligonucleotide or pharmacological treatments.

nodes.description: Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts diaphragm formation and can produce hernia or eventration defects.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic spectrum of STRA6 mutations: from Matthew-Wood syndrome to non-lethal anophthalmia.
evidence.snippet: Matthew-Wood, Spear, PDAC or MCOPS9 syndrome are alternative names used to refer to combinations of microphthalmia/anophthalmia, malformative cardiac defects, pulmonary dysgenesis, and diaphragmatic hernia.

nodes.description: Reduced embryonic retinoid availability in the developing eye disrupts ocular morphogenesis and contributes to anophthalmia or microphthalmia.

evidence.reference_title: RBP4 disrupts vitamin A uptake homeostasis in a STRA6-deficient animal model for Matthew-Wood syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Loss-of-function analysis in zebrafish embryos revealed that Stra6 deficiency caused vitamin A deprivation of the developing eyes.

nodes.description: Variable congenital heart malformations are part of the characteristic STRA6-related multisystem phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Microphthalmia Syndrome 9: Case Report of a Newborn Baby with Pulmonary Hypoplasia, Diaphragmatic Eventration, Microphthalmia, Cardiac Defect and Severe Primary Pulmonary Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: We report a case of a full-term living male infant with pulmonary hypoplasia, left diaphragmatic eventration, bilateral microphthalmia, congenital cardiac defects, and severe pulmonary hypertension.

nodes.description: Impaired embryonic retinoid signaling disrupts pulmonary development and can lead to pulmonary hypoplasia or agenesis.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation in two Hmong families broadens the range of STRA6-related malformations to include contractures and camptodactyly.
evidence.snippet: PDAC (also termed Matthew Wood) syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by pulmonary hypoplasia/aplasia, diaphragmatic defects, bilateral anophthalmia, and cardiac malformations.

nodes.description: STRA6 deficiency disrupts retinoid homeostasis and perturbs retinoic acid receptor signaling and gene regulation during embryogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: RBP4 disrupts vitamin A uptake homeostasis in a STRA6-deficient animal model for Matthew-Wood syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We provide evidence that, in the absence of Stra6, holo-Rbp4 provokes nonspecific vitamin A excess in several embryonic tissues, impairing retinoic acid receptor signaling and gene regulation.

nodes.description: Bilateral clinical anophthalmia is one of the classic ocular manifestations of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation in two Hmong families broadens the range of STRA6-related malformations to include contractures and camptodactyly.
evidence.snippet: PDAC (also termed Matthew Wood) syndrome is a rare, autosomal recessive disorder characterized by pulmonary hypoplasia/aplasia, diaphragmatic defects, bilateral anophthalmia, and cardiac malformations.

nodes.description: Diaphragmatic defects are common and include congenital diaphragmatic hernia and eventration.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic spectrum of STRA6 mutations: from Matthew-Wood syndrome to non-lethal anophthalmia.
evidence.snippet: Matthew-Wood, Spear, PDAC or MCOPS9 syndrome are alternative names used to refer to combinations of microphthalmia/anophthalmia, malformative cardiac defects, pulmonary dysgenesis, and diaphragmatic hernia.

nodes.description: STRA6 encodes a cell-surface receptor and transporter for retinol bound to plasma retinol-binding protein. Biallelic STRA6 deficiency impairs cellular vitamin A uptake as the initiating molecular defect in the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: RBP4 disrupts vitamin A uptake homeostasis in a STRA6-deficient animal model for Matthew-Wood syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The cellular uptake of vitamin A from its RBP4-bound circulating form (holo-RBP4) is a homeostatic process that evidently depends on the multidomain membrane protein STRA6.

evidence.reference_title: A novel mutation in two Hmong families broadens the range of STRA6-related malformations to include contractures and camptodactyly.
evidence.snippet: The disorder is caused by mutations in STRA6, an important regulator of vitamin A and retinoic acid metabolism.

nodes.description: Some affected individuals have bilateral microphthalmia rather than complete anophthalmia.

evidence.reference_title: Microphthalmia Syndrome 9: Case Report of a Newborn Baby with Pulmonary Hypoplasia, Diaphragmatic Eventration, Microphthalmia, Cardiac Defect and Severe Primary Pulmonary Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: We report a case of a full-term living male infant with pulmonary hypoplasia, left diaphragmatic eventration, bilateral microphthalmia, congenital cardiac defects, and severe pulmonary hypertension.

nodes.description: Pulmonary hypoplasia or agenesis is a hallmark extracocular feature and a major driver of perinatal severity.

evidence.reference_title: Microphthalmia Syndrome 9: Case Report of a Newborn Baby with Pulmonary Hypoplasia, Diaphragmatic Eventration, Microphthalmia, Cardiac Defect and Severe Primary Pulmonary Hypertension.
evidence.snippet: We report a case of a full-term living male infant with pulmonary hypoplasia, left diaphragmatic eventration, bilateral microphthalmia, congenital cardiac defects, and severe pulmonary hypertension.

evidence.reference_title: Matthew-Wood syndrome is caused by truncating mutations in the retinol-binding protein receptor gene STRA6.
evidence.snippet: The fetuses had either a homozygous insertion/deletion in exon 2 or a homozygous insertion in exon 7 predicting a premature stop codon in STRA6 transcripts.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic spectrum of STRA6 mutations: from Matthew-Wood syndrome to non-lethal anophthalmia.
evidence.snippet: Among these patients, six novel mutations were identified, bringing the current total of known STRA6 mutations to seventeen.
